ksa_text: Knowledge of data monetization models and data product strategy, including internal data products, external data licensing, data-enabled services, and data marketplace design principles.

dcwf.top_element_label: Knowledge of resource management principles and techniques.

ddat.top_element_label: Capability building for digital, data and technology

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Included: knowledge maps directly to Chief Data and Information Officer scope — GOV-specific signals detected ("data   :monetiz|valuation|product|asset management"). STRM composite is moderate-to-strong (0.4082). Domain Leadership & Strategy (affinity 0.88) is a natural fit for this governance role. Seniority is appropriate for C-suite executive level.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: External check: no matches in governance job postings or certification curricula (CDMP, DAMA, IAPP CIPP/CIPM, NIST AI RMF, ISO 42001). Would a working Chief Data and Information Officer self-report this as a required competency?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Challenge answered: the governance competency is implicit in Chief Data and Information Officer practice (domain affinity 0.88, 1 GOV signal(s)). Framework vocabulary matching underrepresents many legitimate governance competencies.
